prompts.label: Hismile vs Snow
prompts.query: Hismile vs Snow teeth whitening which is better
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile PAP is gentler and better for sensitivity, while Snow LED is faster-acting for dramatic results; choose based on sensitivity levels. Both are effective, so select based on whether you prioritize comfort (Hismile) or speed (Snow).

prompts.label: Hismile vs Colgate

prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile is premium and gentler for sensitive teeth, while Colgate Optic White is more affordable for daily maintenance. Use Colgate for daily whitening and Hismile for intensive whitening treatments if you want both options.
prompts.gptResponse: Colgate Optic White offers more widespread dental care benefits with the addition of optic brightening, while Hismile focuses on sensitivity-free whitening with its PAP+ technology.

prompts.label: Hismile vs Zimba
prompts.query: Hismile vs Zimba teeth whitening kit review
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile PAP is clinically proven and gentler, while Zimba uses charcoal which may be abrasive; Hismile is the safer choice. Both offer premium options, but Hismile has better reviews for sensitive teeth and long-term safety.
prompts.gptResponse: Hismile provides a peroxide-free option which is ideal for sensitive teeth, whereas Zimba offers an effective, cheaper option without specialized sensitivity features.

prompts.label: TikTok whitening
prompts.query: Does that purple teeth whitening serum from TikTok actually work
prompts.claudeResponse: Purple whitening serums (typically from brands like Sheer Smile) work by optical illusion, not actual whitening, and may contain unregulated ingredients. Stick to proven brands like Snow, Hismile, or Crest for actual whitening results.
prompts.gptResponse: The purple teeth whitening serum, such as V34 Color Corrector Serum, helps neutralize yellow tones but provides more of an illusion of whitening rather than a true chemical change.

prompts.label: Whitening results
prompts.query: Honest review of peroxide-free teeth whitening systems
prompts.claudeResponse: Peroxide-free systems like Hismile PAP and activated charcoal toothpastes work but deliver slower results than peroxide-based products. They're better for sensitivity but expect 4-8 weeks for noticeable results compared to 1-2 weeks with peroxide.
